methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $66M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Bright Machines
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Elementary Robotics is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Arye Barnehama that has raised $66M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 private companies at same or adjacent stages—Exotec ($2.0B Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B Series C)—alongside one public comparable.

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression, using total raised ($66M) as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C-to-next-stage advancement, yields $112M. Peer-median analysis across the five robotics and manufacturing comparables produces a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $2.0B. The dispersion reflects the heterogeneity of capital efficiency and product maturity across the sample.

Triangulating these methods produces a wide implied range: $95M (low), $881M (median), and $1.9B (high). The gap between stage-progression and peer-median reflects Elementary's relative youth in capital deployment versus later-stage comparables. Key uncertainties include whether Elementary's product traction and unit economics merit placement toward the upper end of the peer distribution, and the extent to which near-term financing conditions may compress multiples industry-wide.
